item.title: Elmeré van der Berg is the buy of the Super Netball season, but she almost signed for the opposing grand final team
item.description: South African import Elmeré van der Berg has scored more goals than anyone else in her debut Super Netball season. Ahead of the grand final she speaks about her 'resting bitch face', retraining her brain to embrace weight gain, and the tweaks the Adelaide Thunderbirds have made to take her game to another level.

item.title: Papua separatists kill US pilot amid claim Indonesian troops were being brought to region
item.description: A Papua separatist group has shot dead an American pilot who allegedly transported Indonesian troops into the restive province.

item.title: Solomon Islands PM says 'growing consensus' in support of regional security agreement
item.description: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale says there is "growing consensus" in support of his proposal for a Pacific-wide security agreement.

item.title: Gary Glitter charged with historical child sex offences
item.description: The charges were linked to alleged sexual offences against a child dating back to 1978, British prosecutors said.

item.title: Tougher penalties, but no gun limit under Tasmania's proposed new firearms laws post-Bondi
item.description: New gun laws, which create tougher penalties for the theft and possession of stolen firearms, but do not include gun caps, have been unveiled by the Tasmanian government, asking for public feedback.

item.title: Remote Indigenous artists travel to Sydney for National Indigenous Art Fair
item.description: Remote Indigenous artists from around Australia are in Sydney for the annual National Indigenous Art Fair.

item.title: West Coast Eagles star's 'grieving best mate' pens open letter to AFL leaders calling on them to act on CTE
item.description: In a five-page letter to the executives and commissioners of the AFL, a grieving best mate has slammed the organisation for "failing" former West Coasts player Adam Hunter, who suffered from CTE.

item.title: Man rescued from flood waters as clean-up continues from SA storm damage
item.description: Storm damage have prompted extensive clean-ups across South Australia while a man had to be rescued from his vehicle's rooftop after he tried to drive through flood waters.

item.title: How to keep your home warm if you're renting or on a budget
item.description: If you're searching for ways to make your home warmer, these easy and inexpensive tricks can make a big difference.

item.title: Police investigate shooting deaths of two horses in Kosciuszko National Park
item.description: A police investigation is underway after the discovery of two wild horse carcasses in Kosciuszko National Park.

item.title: Lawrence Springborg responds to reports he is tipped to become Queensland governor
item.description: Lawrence Springborg says he has not asked for, sought, nor been offered the role of governor, who represents King Charles in Queensland.

item.title: DarkLab ditches Transformer artwork in Huon Valley due to escalating costs
item.description: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

item.title: NT shadow minister Manuel Brown admits underage son drove car, considers resignation from portfolios
item.description: Manuel Brown has admitted his underage son drove his personal car, one week after failing to disclose the matter when asked by the media, and says he is considering resigning from his shadow portfolios.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: A July 4 special with an alien invasion, George III on TV and Taylor Swift
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Pokémon GO was a 2016 phenomenon. Ten years later, some players never stopped
item.description: One Pokémon GO fan walked 32,000 km in his search to "catch them all". On the way he found health, friendship and a partner.

item.title: Will Conroy's cat carcass distract from Labor's AUKUS conundrum?
item.description: After guiding the country through a tumultuous six months, the Albanese government faces tough fiscal and political decisions.

item.title: Australia has strengthened ties with Indonesia but our Asia capability is in decline
item.description: As Asian language enrolments wither Australia is failing to make lasting ties within our region.

item.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic
item.description: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed, spokesperson confirms
item.description: A heavy media presence and barricades surrounded the Madison Square Garden stadium where fans waited in sweltering conditions.

item.title: Osprey chicks hatch on Daintree live stream
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bird flu has arrived after months of algal bloom, but primary industries minister says SA is ready
item.description: While the arrival of bird flu on SA shores is a "huge concern", the state's primary industries minister maintains the government is prepared for the possibility of a wider local outbreak.

item.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce as fans celebrate America's 'royal wedding'
item.description: Pop superstar Taylor Swift and her partner Travis Kelce have married at a ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler.

item.title: Great Barrier Reef avoids 'in danger' listing in World Heritage Committee draft decision
item.description: The UN body raised "utmost concern" over the site's declining coral cover after mass bleaching events in 2024 and 2025.

item.title: Four rescued after 4WD swept into flooded Macalister River crossing in Gippsland
item.description: Four people are rescued from floodwaters after their 4WD is swept into a river crossing.

item.title: Labor's Meaghan Scanlon won't rule out seat-swap after boundary redraw
item.description: The independent redistribution commission has recommended a substantial redraw of the seat of Gaven, which Meaghan Scanlon reclaimed at the last state election with a very slim margin.

item.title: We go behind the scenes to discover the secrets of Play School's 60-year success
item.description: Australian Story is given rare behind-the-scenes access to Play School as it celebrates 60 years on air. An intimate look at the creative process of the iconic children's TV show that's still guided by one golden rule.

item.title: Australian influencer Lily Jay's tangled web of AI manipulation
item.description: Receiving millions of views online, the Lily Jay Foundation claims to help children across the world. She was even given an award for her work. But what is real and what is AI-generated? ABC NEWS Verify investigates.

item.title: Socceroos match shows where Labor's gambling ad laws fall short
item.description: There should be rules against jumping on the World Cup bandwagon to illustrate a political point, but the Socceroos' valiant effort against Egypt offers a timely real-world test.

item.title: Taylor Swift's 'royal wedding' was a lesson in media mastery
item.description: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

item.title: Wimbledon shock as Filipino Alexandra Eala makes history against Iga Świątek
item.description: Alexandra Eala is left in tears after her stunning victory over reigning Wimbledon champion Iga Świątek.

item.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration
item.description: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

item.title: Greyhound injuries, deaths kept offline out of 'respect' for trainers and 'their animals', Tasracing says
item.description: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

item.title: Maintenance issues, outages plague WA standalone power systems
item.description: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

item.title: International experts say surveilling local birds, marine mammals a 'priority' in bird flu response
item.description: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

item.title: Banker-turned-barefoot mosaic artist and ceramicist spend six years recreating Klimt's The Kiss
item.description: When a bank manager discovered mosaics, he threw away his shoes and tie and vowed never to wear them again.

item.title: Liberal frontbencher Dan Tehan says coalition with One Nation not 'being talked about'
item.description: Shadow energy minister Dan Tehan says the Liberal party is not entertaining thoughts of forming a coalition with One Nation.

item.title: Geraldton band Ratsalad gains global renown with Jackass movie song
item.description: Geraldton band Ratsalad thought they were being scammed when the producer of the latest Jackass movie said they wanted to feature their song in the credits.

item.title: Father, daughter sail Newcastle to Norway returning yacht to its roots
item.description: A NSW family is returning a wooden yacht to its roots in Norway, but it has not been all smooth sailing.

item.title: Youth offenders face mandatory jail time for breaching bail under expanded 'adult crime, adult time' policy
item.description: The expanded "adult crime, adult time" laws would see youth offenders who commit serious crimes while on bail facing a mandatory jail sentence.

item.title: Statue of former St Kilda player Nicky Winmar removed from Perth Stadium after assault conviction
item.description: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

item.title: Ebola treatment trial begins in Congo as outbreak continues to grow
item.description: A trial for an experimental Ebola treatment starts in Congo, where the largest-ever Ebola outbreak continues to grow.

item.title: Fears for Gaza doctor Hussam Abu Safiya detained in Israel
item.description: Hussam Abu Safiya's lawyers believe he is at risk of being killed behind bars in an Israeli prison.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 6: Who quit Pearl Jam last year and what do you know about Wimbledon?
item.description: Let us test your smarts about Captain James Cook's last voyage, the debut release of a popular British girl group and other historical events.

item.title: Race Around the World recap: Catch the week five films
item.description: From manga to life-sized dolls, catch up on the films from week five of Race Around the World.

item.title: Gutsy England prevails over Mexico in instant World Cup classic
item.description: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through.

item.title: AFP to push Australia's Pacific police training model to reduce UN costs
item.description: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

item.title: Domestic and family violence victim-survivors at risk of CTE, experts warn
item.description: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signs Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji
item.description: Australia and Fiji strike a new defence alliance in a major development that could reshape the Pacific's strategic landscape.

item.title: World Cup: US striker Folarin Balogun freed to play Belgium despite red card after Donald Trump call
item.description: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

item.title: Mental health service supports new NT parents struggling with depression and anxiety
item.description: Natalya Wallace thought she just had the baby blues as a result of a traumatic birth experience. When she realised she needed mental health support, the young mother was relieved to find a new service in her rural area.

item.title: China to test ballistic missile in Pacific — as it happened
item.description: The Chinese government informed governments in the region about the test earlier today, the ABC has been told.

item.title: Heritage sheep breeder, 11, helping Hampshire Down variety avoid extinction
item.description: Mitch Dickens's passion for sheep genetics saw his parents give him his own stud, which they hope will help keep a heritage breed alive.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Naomi Osaka stuns Aryna Sabalenka to advance to quarterfinals
item.description: Naomi Osaka downs top seed Aryna Sabalenka in straight sets in a fourth round meeting of grand slam champions at Wimbledon.

item.title: Anthony Albanese 'unequivocally' apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue during podcast game
item.description: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic beats Roger Federer record despite 'meltdowns'
item.description: Novak Djokovic fights off a plucky opponent and his own demons to etch his name into another corner of the record books at Wimbledon.

item.title: WA minister Paul Papalia quits politics, triggering Secret Harbour by-election
item.description: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

item.title: Humpback whale freed from fishing gear on NSW Central Coast
item.description: Whale rescuers say there have been 24 entanglements already this migration season. Meanwhile, a small whale was euthanised after it stranded itself on a beach near Newcastle, despite rescue efforts from residents and experts.

item.title: Nigel Farage referred to UK standards watchdog over undeclared gifts from crypto investor George Cottrell
item.description: Nigel Farage was reportedly provided with security services, social media support and accommodation by George Cottrell, a 32-year-old crypto investor who previously served eight months in prison for wire fraud.

item.title: Rare humpback whale calf, believed to be a leucistic, spotted off NSW and Queensland coast
item.description: The calf, thought to be one of only a dozen in the Southern Hemisphere, has been spotted swimming alongside its mother.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Erling Haaland surprises himself
item.description: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: 'Destabilising' Chinese ballistic missile test in Pacific prompts government concerns
item.description: China has test-fired a long-range missile with a dummy warhead in the Pacific Ocean, prompting concerns from the Australian government.

item.title: Four of our best self-saucing puddings for winter
item.description: You'd never guess it but self-saucing puddings are so easy to make. Here are four of our best for you try while the weather is cool.

item.title: Mabil says monoculture debate 'bulls***' as Socceroos return home from FIFA World Cup
item.description: The Socceroos star says anyone "trying to divide" the community is "probably not Australian themselves".

item.title: 'Pink army' gathers in Rundle Mall to welcome home victorious Thunderbirds
item.description: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

item.title: ACT public school language education plan found 'not fit for purpose' by parliamentary committee
item.description: The ACT government's blueprint to deliver language education in Canberra public schools is "not fit for purpose", a parliamentary committee finds, prompting calls for the government to once again deliver a centralised ATAR-level language learning plan.

item.title: Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review US player's red card, as UEFA says U-turn 'crossed a red line'
item.description: Football's European governing body blasts the decision to suspend a US striker's World Cup red-card ban, saying FIFA had "crossed a red line" after US President Donald Trump personally intervened in the case.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

item.title: Use your phone for work? This is how you can claim it as a tax deduction
item.description: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

item.title: Too cold to exercise? Here's how to start and stay on track
item.description: Experts say winter is a good time to double down on exercise rather than taking the foot off the gas.

item.title: World Cup: 'Shameful', incomprehensible': Severe blowback over FIFA's opaque Balogun decision
item.description: FIFA has always insisted it is politically neutral. But its integrity is now being questioned after US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked for Folarin Balogun's suspension to be reviewed.

item.title: 'Bad blood' between US and European allies as NATO summit begins
item.description: Dozens of world leaders are descending on Türkiye this week to rubber-stamp significant NATO commitments, but the dynamics on the sidelines could prove more interesting to watch.

item.title: SA Museum's 'walking archive' helps First Nations people discover family history
item.description: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

item.title: Queensland mental health services at risk from 'huge' funding shortfall
item.description: Queensland mental health community services receive funding from government departments based on an indexation rate.

item.title: Alex de Minaur loses in Wimbledon fourth round to Flavio Cobolli
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses in the fourth round at Wimbledon in another match he feels he could, and maybe should, have won against ninth seed Flavio Cobolli.

item.title: Alcoa's looming WA alumina monopoly could threaten world's only jarrah forests, environmentalists say
item.description: Alcoa's $8 billion purchase of South32 could be key to unlocking untouched areas of WA's northern jarrah forest for bauxite mining, raising the concern of environmentalists.

item.title: Alexandra Eala makes another stride for Philippines as Jasmine Paolini ends her Wimbledon run
item.description: For a nation like the Philippines, where sports stars are held on an even higher pedestal, Alexandra Eala is taking her place among the greats.

item.title: Kaniva finally connects to drinkable mains water, but not all residents want it on tap
item.description: Victoria's largest town without drinking water finally gets a new potable supply, but it has left the community that has relied on tank water divided.

item.title: Pacific leaders criticise Chinese nuclear-capable missile test as 'reckless'
item.description: Solomon Islands PM says a ballistic missile test carried out by China is "not the act of a friend", sentiments echoed by several Pacific leaders.

item.title: Paul Papalia's difficult departure leaves WA Labor nervous about One Nation's surge
item.description: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner accused of sexual assault
item.description: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

item.title: Former NRL player Jai Arrow marries partner months after motor neurone disease diagnosis
item.description: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

item.title: With one missile, China sends world a reminder of its growing military power
item.description: This latest military test demonstrates China's growing willingness to use its military strength to send a warning to the Pacific: that Beijing now has the military and nuclear means to assert its dominance in the region.

item.title: Government commits to restore current Paradise Dam wall over new build
item.description: The Queensland government announces a plan to rebuild the Paradise Dam wall where it is, despite its operator previously saying there were too many issues to fix it.

item.title: Fishing ban lifted early in SA amid positive signs for King George whiting
item.description: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

item.title: World Cup: Belgium consoles Folarin Balogun but claims 'justice' was served in 4-1 win over US
item.description: Belgium did not feel it was fair Folarin Balogun played for the US in the round of 16 match after he was given a red card in his team's previous match.

item.title: Suspected bird flu identified in giant petrel on SA coast, but testing to determine if H5
item.description: A suspected H5 bird flu case has been detected in a giant petrel on SA's Yorke Peninsula, but the state government says the viral load is low, with further results expected within the next 48 hours.

item.title: Report suggesting DPP Sally Dowling be removed over media leak condemned by attorney-general
item.description: The inquiry into children's identity protection was called in October last year, but has focused almost solely on the leaking of the story to media.

item.title: Is FIFA’s integrity at stake after Donald Trump’s interference?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Ukrainian prosecutors confirm Monaco bomb suspect found dead
item.description: A Ukrainian woman suspected of a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian-born businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Ukraine, according to Ukraine's prosecutor general's office.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage quits as an MP amid donation probe and vows to win by-election
item.description: Nigel Farage, the far-right political leader riding a surge of support in Britain, has announced a major gamble that could end his political career.

item.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight
item.description: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout.

item.title: Donald Trump lashes out at NATO leaders, while $72bn in arms deals struck at summit
item.description: In a sign they are heeding calls from the United States to spend more to defend Europe, NATO leaders agree to deals worth about $72 billion.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 8: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world
item.description: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

item.title: Deadly podcasts to listen to this NAIDOC Week, from Indigenuity to Inside the Huddle
item.description: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

item.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
item.description: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

item.title: Video shows men who attended Khamenei's funeral and the son who didn't
item.description: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men it sees as central to it.

item.title: What time is State of Origin III kick-off? How to watch the Maroons vs Blues decider
item.description: The men's State of Origin series will be decided tonight, as Queensland hosts New South Wales at Lang Park in a winner-takes-all Game III. Here is what you need to know ahead of kick-off.

item.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
item.description: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

item.title: Outback becomes oasis of birds, wildlife after 'unbelievable' desert rains
item.description: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.

item.title: CFA senior leaders accidentally send 'draft' response to fatal bushfire inquiry
item.description: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

item.title: Telstra outage: 'Largely returned to business as usual' after nationwide crash
item.description: Communications Minister Anika Wells says Telstra has "largely returned to business as usual" after its network suffered a nationwide outage that impacted potentially millions of Australians.

item.title: Seventeen dead, hundreds injured as China braces for more wild weather
item.description: China's president calls for "all out" rescue efforts after storms kill at least 17 people and force the evacuation of tens of thousands.

item.title: Iran targets sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after wave of US strikes
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards target US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after the US launched a wave of military strikes on Iran.

item.title: Naomi Osaka says she's getting old after Wimbledon quarterfinal loss to Karolina Muchova
item.description: Japan's Naomi Osaka bemoans getting old as her inspiring Wimbledon run comes to an end in the quarterfinals.

item.title: World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan fumes at ref after Argentina loss
item.description: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side "suffered an injustice" in defeat to Argentina, raging at the referee for a number of perceived errors.

item.title: European politicians launch bid for investigation into FIFA boss Gianni Infantino red card controversy
item.description: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

item.title: Inside Guts: the solar-powered music tour connecting remote NT communities and Australian bands
item.description: Behind the scenes of the Guts tour: a roaming, solar-powered tour that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush to hold concerts in areas that don't host a lot of touring musicians.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Egypt coach blows up over 'fixed' tournament as Belgium trolls Trump
item.description: An all-time blow-up from Egypt's coach headlines the day at the World Cup, as the fallout from Belgium's win over the US continues. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Four fined after allegedly illegally fishing in green zone during Queensland competition
item.description: The group was caught allegedly fishing illegally in a no-take zone during a competition. Four people have been fined almost $15,000.

item.title: CLP politician Jo Hersey claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns in Darwin CBD
item.description: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

item.title: BHP staff confirm historic strike action at mining giant's Port Hedland operations
item.description: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

item.title: Hikers warned to take Adelaide's peri-urban parks seriously after rescue
item.description: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

item.title: Sale of Tasmania's biggest farm, Rushy Lagoon, to UK pine plantation investor approved
item.description: The sale of Tasmania's biggest farm to the UK's largest forestry investor has been given the green light — with the news greeted by outrage from the state's farmers' group.

item.title: Former NSW Liberal minister John Sidoti charged with misconduct over property redevelopment pressure
item.description: Former New South Wales Liberal government minister John Sidoti says he will fight a charge of misconduct while in public office.

item.title: The devastation of ACL injuries can get lost in how prolific they are, says Essendon's Emily Gough
item.description: Essendon AFLW forward Emily Gough said devastating ACL injuries can get lost in the sheer amount of them in women's game.

item.title: Australian woman arrested in US after allegedly voting illegally in federal elections
item.description: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

item.title: World Cup quarterfinals: When and where as France, England and Argentina take next step
item.description: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.

item.title: Investigation into former NACC commissioner Paul Brereton discontinued over cost
item.description: Investigations into allegations against Paul Brereton will end after the anti-corruption inspector found they would be too expensive to pursue.

item.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says
item.description: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

item.title: How the Telstra outage impacted these Australians
item.description: Millions have potentially been impacted by the Telstra outage. Here's how it affected Australians doing basic everyday things, from making calls to measuring blood sugar levels.

item.title: Timeline shows delay before Telstra alerted minister to network outage
item.description: Telstra has defended its communication process, insisting the government and other stakeholders were informed "very, very early" in the morning.

item.title: Why did China launch its missile test?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: State of Origin quick hits: Jai Arrow presents shield as Queensland dudded by bunker
item.description: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

item.title: Australians face record-high rents across all capital cities: Domain report
item.description: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

item.title: Ruth Ellis receives pardon from king 70 years after hanging in UK
item.description: The case of Ruth Ellis has gripped Britain for decades and inspired the 1985 film Dance with a Stranger.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran is 'scum' as Ukraine gets big Patriot missile win at wild NATO summit
item.description: Donald Trump says his country will probably continue to attack Iran and says Ukraine will be given permission to manufacture much-needed Patriot missiles as it fights Russia's full-scale invasion.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list
item.description: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

item.title: TV quote quiz: Guess the shows, then put them in order
item.description: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

item.title: Increased demand and new varieties set Australian coffee industry up for expansion
item.description: Australians are among the world's biggest coffee drinkers but less than 1 per cent of the coffee consumed nationwide is grown locally. That could be about to change.

item.title: Live entertainment industry calls for government lifeline as more musicals close
item.description: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

item.title: US carries out strikes on Iran after Donald Trump declares ceasefire 'over'
item.description: The United States military carries out renewed strikes on Iran aimed at further degrading its ability to "threaten freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz", after US President Donald Trump earlier threatened a "big attack" was coming.

item.title: Questions asked about telco regulation after Telstra suffers outage
item.description: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

item.title: How snow machines have become the key ingredient for ski seasons
item.description: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

item.title: Queensland has the most motorcycle deaths in Australia for five years, and the numbers are rising in 2026
item.description: Police say they've seen a rise in anti-social hooning behaviour as Queensland records a rise in fatal motorcycle crashes.

item.title: Anthony Albanese says India a 'top-tier security partner' for Australia as nations deepen defence ties
item.description: Anthony Albanese says Australia will increase coordination with India's military forces following talks with the nation's Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Melbourne.

item.title: World Cup: Racist commentary called out in media coverage, hurting diversity at the top
item.description: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

item.title: Iran says it has struck US bases after Trump restarts attacks — as it happened
item.description: Tehran says it has struck US military infrastructure in the Middle East after Washington launched a fresh series of strikes against Iran to "degrade their ability to threaten freedom of navigation" in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: NT Aboriginal language speakers call for stronger language revival efforts
item.description: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

item.title: V/Line train services slowly resume in Victoria following Telstra outage
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan has promised two days of free travel on regional rail networks following Wednesday's standstill.

item.title: SA police investigating possible Telstra outage death — as it happened
item.description: South Australian police confirm they are investigating a death possibly linked to Telstra outage. Look back at the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

item.title: Why a Telstra outage has stopped some trains in regional New South Wales
item.description: Trains relying on the 4G network of freight rail track operator ARTC went down amid the Telstra failure.

item.title: Five First Nations musicians redefining classical music in Australia
item.description: From James Henry to Nardi Simpson, Aaron Wyatt, Adam Manning and Nina Korbe, here are First Nations voices making waves in Australia’s classical music scene.

item.title: Researchers harvest water from the air to grow native seedlings and rehydrate the Wheatbelt
item.description: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

item.title: Footage showing ruins of Khamenei's compound released
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Emmys 2026: Widow's Bay sneaks in, Rob Reiner honoured and more Emmy surprises
item.description: Plus Connor Storrie nabs his first nom but not for Heated Rivalry, Hacks soars on a swan song and more successes, surprises and snubs from today's Emmy nominations.

item.title: The Desert Champions Way: Outback Camel Trail, 2026, starts with camel races in Jundah
item.description: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.

item.title: Search for alternatives to paraquat continues after usage restricted
item.description: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

item.title: Trump says US is hitting Iran 'very hard'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner suspends midterm campaign after sexual assault accusation
item.description: The Democratic Senate candidate in the US state of Maine has suspended his midterm election campaign after a string of controversies, including an accusation of sexual assault.

item.title: Envoy to combat antisemitism calls for independent oversight committee to review ABC's compliance with charter
item.description: The ABC and SBS were called to give evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, with the inquiry hearing that a number of complaints had been received about the public broadcasters' coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
item.description: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

item.title: US strikes Iran and reinstates oil sanctions, but Tehran has already raked in billions
item.description: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

item.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
item.description: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

item.title: Our Watch condemns Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence spike on State of Origin night
item.description: A leading violence prevention organisation says an Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence is unacceptable.

item.title: Trainee surgeon Ryan Cho pleads guilty to secretly filming hundreds of victims
item.description: Ryan Cho has pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

item.title: Life returns to streets around damaged NYC high-rise
item.description: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

item.title: How Hudson Young and Liam Martin bashed their way to State of Origin glory
item.description: Liam Martin and Hudson Young don't like to point fingers, unless it comes time to decide which of the New South Wales backrowers is madder.

item.title: Swimming Australia to take proactive, world-first approach to 'scary' fake news stories
item.description: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

item.title: Search continues after Boeing cargo plane crashes off coast of Pakistan
item.description: Rescue crews battle rough sea conditions to recover parts of a Boeing cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea. Five crew members remain missing.

item.title: Telstra outage leaves elderly woman with dementia 'frightened', as senator's comments labelled 'irresponsible'
item.description: An SA woman says her elderly mother was left "frightened" during the Telstra outage after a fall. Meanwhile, SA police have said they have no record of any deaths during the network failure, despite a senator's claims.

item.title: More than 1,300 staff receive $2 million in back pay from disability support provider Yooralla
item.description: Fair Work Ombudsman Anna Booth says Yooralla's underpayment of staff serves as a warning to employers to comply with minimum payment laws.

item.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury
item.description: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

item.title: Court told former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire 'kept in dark' over fraudulent visa scheme
item.description: Daryl Maguire's barrister has told a jury it was "unbelievable" to think the ex NSW MP would recruit people in his electorate to take part in an allegedly fraudulent visa scheme.

item.title: This Place: Koala Brother, Yarriabini
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: State of Origin Game III the highest-rating program in series history
item.description: An unprecedented number of viewers tuned into the State of Origin decider, in which NSW stunned Queensland to win the series.

item.title: From TikTok to podcasts, politics is chasing attention but there's a downside
item.description: Every press conference, reel and podcast is now a chance for politicians to connect with voters. Yet the strategy that broadens an MP's reach can also amplify their biggest mistakes.

item.title: NSW Police officer taken into custody after sexually assaulting, choking then-wife
item.description: A NSW Police officer has been taken into custody after his bail was revoked by a judge, two days after he was found guilty of choking and sexually assaulting his then-wife.

item.title: Cairns man who dissolved wife's body in wheelie bin of acid loses appeal bid for earlier release
item.description: Klaus Andres was sentenced to life in prison in 2013 after he was found guilty of murdering his 42-year-old wife Li Ping Cao.

item.title: Angus Taylor says One Nation policies would bring 'eternity of pain' to economy
item.description: In his strongest remarks pushing back on One Nation, the opposition leader has accused the right-wing party of being a "column of smoke".

item.title: Canberra Hospital opens Australia's first centre dedicated to treating complex immune diseases
item.description: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

item.title: NAIDOC celebrates strength of Warumungu culture with gallery opening in Tennant Creek
item.description: Hundreds of people walked through the streets of Tennant Creek today as part of a NAIDOC march, finishing at the official reopening of the town's major First Nations cultural hub.

item.title: NT children's commissioner resigns as CLP's controversial child protection changes greenlit by scrutiny committee
item.description: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

item.title: ADF releases footage of missile interceptor test days after Chinese ICBM launch
item.description: The prototype system included a mix of Australian and American technology, with Defence aiming to build a medium-range system to defend against missiles or aircraft bound for Australia.

item.title: Family pleads for information about 'gorgeous' missing Toowoomba mum
item.description: Police are searching for a 30-year-old woman who was last seen in Toowoomba on Tuesday.

item.title: Dockers stun the Swans in last quarter blitz to own the top of the ladder
item.description: Fremantle puts on a show in the last quarter of its clash with Sydney to claim the top of the ladder outright.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of power ballad Total Eclipse of the Heart, dies at 75
item.description: Welsh pop singer Bonnie Tyler, ​famed for 1980s power ballads such as Total Eclipse of the ​Heart and Holding Out ​for a Hero, has died.

item.title: Three NT corrections officers stood down with pay over prisoner escape
item.description: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

item.title: Indian Prime Minister’s visit met with both enthusiasm and protests
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: US and Iran trade fresh strikes, explosions reported near nuclear plant — as it happened
item.description: Jordan's military says it has intercepted eight Iranian missiles fired at the country, in a fresh wave of attacks in the Middle East.

item.title: Narendra Modi given rapturous welcome as he addresses thousands at community event in Melbourne
item.description: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

item.title: Nearly 200 firefighters tackle factory fire in China's east which has killed at least 28
item.description: More than 180 firefighters battled the deadly blaze at a factory in China's 'shoe capital'.

item.title: World leaders gifted vintage revolvers by Erdogan after NATO summit
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogansaid the gifts were intended as a showcase of his nation's defence industry.

item.title: Iran targets US military sites in Gulf as Israel warns it could 'strike again'
item.description: Iranian armed forces launched reciprocal attacks on US military infrastructure in Gulf states following US strikes on Iran's south and east regions, straining a three week old ceasefire.

item.title: From giant crab men to searing satire, here is the best TV of 2026 so far
item.description: A mountain of TV has landed in 2026 and we've sifted through it all to deliver you the ones worth your time (or at least a binge-watch).

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: Red cards, ballistic missiles and Pam the Bird
item.description: It's been a week of missile testing, Telstra outages and FIFA World Cup red card interventions. Were you paying attention?

item.title: Hiatus Kaiyote celebrate 15 years as rule-breaking favourites of Beyoncé, Drake, Prince
item.description: They've made admirers out of music's biggest names over their 15-year career, but this Australian group are always thinking about the future.

item.title: The 'stunt' that could cost UK Reform leader Nigel Farage his political career
item.description: Nigel Farage is facing intensifying scrutiny over his finances and millions of dollars' worth of donations. Here's how he got here.

item.title: Telstra recently warned about timing issue linked to national outage
item.description: Government agencies and academics both recently warned Telstra it was vulnerable to the type of error that caused this week's national outage.

item.title: How Beijing 'tripled the size of its nuclear arsenal' in six years
item.description: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

item.title: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific
item.description: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

item.title: AI's opportunities and risks front and centre on Albanese government's agenda
item.description: The government can read the numbers, and one thing they tell us is that without refuelling that vital growth agent, productivity, we will stay in the doldrums.

item.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate
item.description: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

item.title: Why Victoria's transport system was worst impacted by the Telstra outage
item.description: Rail services for regional Victorians have resumed after a Telstra outage caused every line to be shut down for a day and a half. Why was the scale of disruption so severe?

item.title: Africa CDC says Ebola outbreak is 'fastest-growing ever' after 600 deaths since mid-May
item.description: New numbers from health agencies show there have been 1,759 confirmed cases in DR Congo since the outbreak was declared.

item.title: Live: France faces Morocco as World Cup quarterfinals begin with Boston blockbuster
item.description: Kylian Mbappé leads an irresistible French frontline as Les Bleus takes on dark horse Morocco in the first of the World Cup quarterfinals. Follow live.

item.title: Trump's new Iran threats could mean all-out war but there are other options
item.description: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

item.title: The joy of Neil: how a cheeky seal on Tasmania’s shores brought happiness to the world
item.description: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving behind a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

item.title: Proposed laws to extend detention for people in Queensland to three days for mental health check
item.description: The legislation would allow Queensland's chief psychiatrist to extend the maximum detention for a person accused of a serious crime from 24 to 72 hours.

item.title: Queensland campaign to support renters with disability to request home modifications
item.description: About one in three Queenslanders with disability surveyed say they lived in a home that didn't meet their needs last year, a report says.

item.title: You Belong driving program helping refugees find work and connections in Toowoomba
item.description: Learning to drive is helping refugees leave the problems of isolation and difficulty accessing opportunities in the rear-view mirror in Toowoomba.
